pi_items.source: Task description and verifier.py logic explicitly specify and check these four exact track names.
While the description says 'exactly 140', the verifier script explicitly permits a tolerance of 5 BPM.
Task description explicitly specifies an acceptable gain range of -5 to -1 dB for the Upright Bass, checked by verifier.
Task description explicitly specifies an acceptable gain range of -8 to -4 dB for the Drums, checked by verifier.
Task description instructs placing 'at least 3 location markers' which aligns with the verifier checking unique_marker_positions >= 3.
Task description instructs creating a snapshot named 'Pre_Show_Template'. Ardour saves it with the '.ardour' extension, checked by export_result.sh.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on a synthetically generated Ardour session initialized dynamically by environment scripts. All metadata claims exactly match the explicit instructions provided in the prompt and the verification logic present in verifier.py. The required tracks (Upright Bass, Piano, Drums, Saxophone), target tempo (140 BPM), gain ranges for Bass (-5 to -1 dB) and Drums (-8 to -4 dB), the minimum marker count (3), and the snapshot name are fully consistent. The verifier deliberately permits a tempo tolerance of 5 BPM, although the task instructions require 'exactly 140 BPM'.
